edge_labels.source_description: Encoder-decoder RNNs compress the input sequence into one context vector, which limits performance on longer sequences because all input-output communication must pass through that fixed-size representation. A single encoder hidden state is too narrow a bottleneck for long source sentences, motivating selective access to all encoder states during decoding.
edge_labels.target_description: Attention begins by comparing a decoder/query state with each candidate source state, then applying softmax to turn those scores into normalized attention weights. Attention scores can be computed with dot-product, bilinear/multiplicative, low-rank projected dot-product, or additive neural-net scoring.
